units.content_raw:
During the fiscal year 2011/12, the aross profit
of che Commercial Bank of Ethiosia reached
an-all-time high of 7.9 billion Birr. This s an
unprecedented level of profit im CBE’s 70 years
existence.

Tne total income of the Bank was Birr 11.8
billion, up by Birr 4.5 billion (65.5%) when
compared with the performance of the previous
year. At the end of the fiscal year, the total
assets of the Bank stood at Birr 158.1 billion,
while the total deposit reached 120.1 billion
Birt, thus reflecting a 34.9% growth over the
Preceding year's deposit level.

On the other hand, the overall expense of
the Bank reached Birr 3.5 billion, showing
an increase of 32.2%, mainly due ‘to the
substantial increase in interest expense
followed by salaries end benefits. Growth in
general expense was related to the expansion
of CRF.

The Bank hes demonstrated its commitment ta
reach large potential customers and the public
by opening 167 additional branches across
the country in the fiscal year. The opening of
the branches enabled the Bank to increase jts
customer base to about 3.9 milllon and scale
up deposit mobilization efforts.
units.content_markdown:
During the fiscal year 2011/12, the aross profit
of che Commercial Bank of Ethiosia reached
an-all-time high of 7.9 billion Birr. This s an
unprecedented level of profit im CBE’s 70 years
existence.

Tne total income of the Bank was Birr 11.8
billion, up by Birr 4.5 billion (65.5%) when
compared with the performance of the previous
year. At the end of the fiscal year, the total
assets of the Bank stood at Birr 158.1 billion,
while the total deposit reached 120.1 billion
Birt, thus reflecting a 34.9% growth over the
Preceding year's deposit level.

On the other hand, the overall expense of
the Bank reached Birr 3.5 billion, showing
an increase of 32.2%, mainly due ‘to the
substantial increase in interest expense
followed by salaries end benefits. Growth in
general expense was related to the expansion
of CRF.

The Bank hes demonstrated its commitment ta
reach large potential customers and the public
by opening 167 additional branches across
the country in the fiscal year. The opening of
the branches enabled the Bank to increase jts
customer base to about 3.9 milllon and scale
up deposit mobilization efforts.

units.content_raw:
Achieving service excellence and high business
growt are the key objectives of the Commercial
Bank of Ethiopia. To this end, the CBE has
implemented two major projects that are
expected to bring about transformation in its
service delivery and overall performance, These
are Information Technology (17) amd Human
Resources Development (HRD) projects.

Card banking and core banking solutions which
are the components of the major IT projects have
successfully been implemented during the year
under review, The T-24 core banking system went
live on May 2, 2012 with a total of 37 modules
and interfaces going operalional up until the clase
of the fiscal yaar, and the subsequent rollout to
105 branches. The CBE plans to expand the
core banking solutions to 145 more branches in
2012/13; and the number of branches connected
to the system will reach 250 by the end of the
next FY.

The Human Resources Development strategy
has been the other initiatve the bank has been
imp'ementing since 2011, Follawing the agreement
concluded with the Frankfurt School of Finance
ard Management (FSFM) for the development
of CBE's HRD strategy, the strategy cocument
was prepared and its implementation has been

ANNUAL REPORT 2011-12

underway in the fiscal year under review. First
round developmental training was delivered to
management groups of the Bank based on the
competency framewerk developed by the FSFM,

By and large, 2011/12 FY was a very Successful
year for CBE. As usual, customers who continued
to prefer our services were the key drivers for the
successes registered during the year under review.
I would, therefore, like to extend my deepest
appreciation and gratitude to our esteemed
customers.

I would also like to take Lhis opportunity to
sincerely thank the Board of Directors of the CBE
for the visionary leadership they provided, and
the employees of the Bank for their continued
commitment and decication.

With the continuing patronage of our esteemed
customers and the cammitment of the staff, 1 am
very confident that we will have no difficulty in
achieving far better results in the years to come.

BEKALU ZELEKE

President

COMMERCIAL BANK OF ETHIOPIA

n=

1.1

1.2

i2

Macroeconomic Highlights

Global Economy

According to the World Economic
Outlook (IMF, October 2012), with
recovery suffering new setbacks and
growth projections macked down,
world economy has deteriorated
further The key reason for this
is the inability of the policies of
the major advanced economies to
rebuild confidence in medium-term
prospects. The global ‘economic
growth thus remained subdued in
2012, with most regions expanding
at a pace below potential. The
global economy grew at ebout
3.3% in 2012, down from that of
2011 which saw @ 3.8 % growth.
The erisis in Euro-area remains the
most obvious threat to the world
economy. Advanced economies
were projected tc grow by 1.3% in 2012, compared with 1.6% the preceding year and 3.0% in 2010.
Growth in emerging and developing countries also dropped slightly and was expected to reach 5.3%
in 2012; against the recorded 6.2% In 2011.This low growth and uncertainty in advanced economies
is affecting emerging markets and developing economies through bath trade and financial channels
on top of country-specific weaknesses,

Growth in volume of world trade was projected to slump to 3.2% in 2012 from 5.8 % in 2011. The
price of oil remained high in the global market and widely fluctuated owing to economic and political
uncertainties linked with major suppliers. A weak global economic outlook suggested that the nonfuel
commodity orices are unlikely to improve.

The Ethiopian Economy

In 2011/12 FY, the overall economic growth of Ethiopia was 11%. Increased crop production of
smallholder farmers during the main harvest season, efforts to diversify towards irrigation, continued
high investments including public investment in infrastructure and other large agro-processing
projects as well as foreign direct investment are the major drivers of the high growth performance
of the ccanomy. Large public investments and supports to promote micro and small scale industries
also continued to be important sources of employment in the economy,

Efforts to maintain macroeconomic stability through monetary policies, market interventions and
increased supply as well as distributions of basic commodities have contributed to recent improvement
in inflation rate. A notable improvement in the domestic market is the significant fall in the price of
construction materials such as iron and cement,

On the whole, despite some challenges, the Ethicpian economy has shown good performance with
positive cifects on the performance of the financial sector in general and the Commercial Bank of
Ethiopia in particular.

units.content_raw:
ANNUAL REPORT 2011-12

2. Highlights of the CBE

2.1. Financial Position
2.1.1 Net Interest Income

The net interest income of the Bank rose to Birr 5.03 billion in 2011/12, up by 69.9%
from the 2.96 billion Birr in 2010/11, mainly due to higher growth of interest income than
interest expense.

The aggregate interest income grew by 64.2% to Birr 6.7 billlon during the year in review,
This high growth was largely attributed to the increase in the overall disbursement of
loans and advances. Interest expense also increased by Birr 0.56 billion owing to the large
expansion in the deposit base of the bank, including interest bearing deposits.

2.1.2 Net Interest Margin
The net interest margin jumped from 4% in 2010/11 to 4.6% in the year being reviewed.
Net Interest Income (Mn. Birr)

2010/11 | % Change,

Interest Income 6,703.40 — 4,081.50 64.2
Loans and advances 3,607.80) 2,130.80 67.7
Government bonds 27.70) 31.20 =11:2
Treasury bills 0.50

Coupon and corporate bonds 3,053.10 1,893.20 61.3)
Placement with other banks 14.80, 5.80) 155.2 |
Interest expense 1,676.40 1117.20 | 50.1
Customers’ deposits 1,662.60 1,122 | 43,5
Deposits from other banks 0.2 0.3 | i ere
Other 13.6 4.7) | 189.4
Net interest income 5,027.00 2,964.30 | | 69.6

2.1.3.Non-Interest Income

Non-interest income accounted for 42% of the total income. During the yeerunder review,
total non-interest income was Birr 4.8 billion, depicting a 67.2 % increase over the orevious
year. This was mainly due to the surge in income from service charges. Consequently, non-
interest income continued to contribute considerably to the total income of the Bank

2.2. Balance Sheet Analysis

2.2.1 Asset Structure

The total asset of the Bank yrew by 38.4%, and reached Birr 158.1 billion during the fiscal
year under review. The major account categories that pushed the asset balance up were
loan and advance to customers, investments, other assets, ard property end equipment.

Moreover, the asset quality of the Bank .a5 shown successive improvements over recent
years. The Non-Performing Loans (NPLs) ratio shrank to 0.61%, mainly as a result of
the reduction of NPLs stock and to some extent due to increased level of outstanding
loans. The continued high domestic economic performance has notably led to a General
improvement in the jaan repayment capacities of customers.

2.2.2 Loan Portfolio

At the end of the fiscal year, the total outstanding loans and advances of the Bank (including
bonds} rose by 62.9%, reaching 123 billion Birr. Qutstanding loans to customers q-ew by
about 71%,

Compared with the preceding year, the import letters of credit opened and settled during the
reporting year went up by 2.4% and 109.2%, respectively, Gn the other hand, the value of export
documents negotiated cn a collection basis grew by 72.23%, while export documents negotiated
at sight dropped by 4.2%, The value of guarantees issuec plummeted to 93.5% during the year
under review,

Human Resotirces
Cognizant of the fact that strengthening of the human resources is ceatral to organizational
success, CBE concluded an agreement for the development of its human resources

2.5.

development strategy with the Frankfurt School of Finance and Management on January 1,
2011, The strategy document was prepared as per the agreed schedule and approved by the
Board of Directors. Implementation continued in the year under review, during which tre
consultent developed detailed competency gaps on the basis of which training curriculum
have been developed.

In addition, the Bank provided various on-the-job and off-the-job training end recruitec
addilional new employees and trained them. Accordingly, a total of 9.705 employees were
offered with various training programs and specia emphasis was given on recruiting qualified
professionals, In the reporting period, it recruited 2,511 permanent employees, raising the
workforce of CBE ta 12,787 hy the end of the FY. It also retained the existing staff through
devising different incentives and benefit packages,

Business Development

The 2011/12 financial year saw the opening of additional 167 branches, bringing the Bank's
branch network to 547, Many of the existing branches also expanded their services by opening
additional windows, Consequently, the customer base grew significantly, reaching 3.9 million
at the end of June 2011/12. In addition, 50 ATMs were installed at the end of June 2011 in
the major city branches, hotels, airport and commercial centers in Addis Ababa, providing
24/7 services for both damestic end VISA/ATM card holders,

Apart from increased accessibility, impressive progress was also registered with respect to the
implementation of IT projects such as card banking and integrated core banking solutions,
and the installation of infrastructure during the fiscal year. The piloting of mobile top up and
bill payment Services has been launched ard cammercialization of mobile top upis underway.

Cognizant of the fact that strengthening of the human resources is ceatral to organizational
success, CBE concluded an agreement for the development of its human resources

2.5.

development strategy with the Frankfurt School of Finance and Management on January 1,
2011, The strategy document was prepared as per the agreed schedule and approved by the
Board of Directors. Implementation continued in the year under review, during which tre
consultent developed detailed competency gaps on the basis of which training curriculum
have been developed.

In addition, the Bank provided various on-the-job and off-the-job training end recruitec
addilional new employees and trained them. Accordingly, a total of 9.705 employees were
offered with various training programs and specia emphasis was given on recruiting qualified
professionals, In the reporting period, it recruited 2,511 permanent employees, raising the
workforce of CBE ta 12,787 hy the end of the FY. It also retained the existing staff through
devising different incentives and benefit packages,

Business Development

The 2011/12 financial year saw the opening of additional 167 branches, bringing the Bank's
branch network to 547, Many of the existing branches also expanded their services by opening
additional windows, Consequently, the customer base grew significantly, reaching 3.9 million
at the end of June 2011/12. In addition, 50 ATMs were installed at the end of June 2011 in
the major city branches, hotels, airport and commercial centers in Addis Ababa, providing
24/7 services for both damestic end VISA/ATM card holders,

Apart from increased accessibility, impressive progress was also registered with respect to the
implementation of IT projects such as card banking and integrated core banking solutions,
and the installation of infrastructure during the fiscal year. The piloting of mobile top up and
bill payment Services has been launched ard cammercialization of mobile top upis underway.
